entries.title: Dealix Human Review Statement
entries.preview:
# Dealix Human Review Statement

Every outbound action involving the customer's brand, prospects, or money requires explicit human approval before it leaves the workspace.

## Approval matrix

| Action | Reviewer | Tool |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Outreach draft → outbound | Founder | `scripts/approve_outreach_draft.py` |
| Outreach draft → rejected | Founder | `scripts/reject_outreach_draft.py` |
| Quote → sent to customer | Founder + customer's commercial owner | `scripts/approve_quote.py` |
| Dea

entries.title: أسئلة المشترين المؤسسيين — Dealix
entries.preview:
# أسئلة المشترين المؤسسيين — Dealix

## ما هو Dealix؟

Dealix شركة سعودية تبني أنظمة تشغيل تجارية (Operating Systems) فوق أدواتك الحالية: CRM، واتساب، السمعة، البلاغات، الفواتير. ما نستبدل أنظمتك — نوصلها ونحوكمها وننتج إثباتات.

## ما الفرق بين Dealix ووكالة تسويق؟

الوكالة تنفذ حملات. Dealix يبني نظام تشغيل يستمر بعد انتهاء العقد. نسلّم تدفقات، ولوحات، وموافقات، وتقارير إثبات — مش حملة فردية.

## هل تستعملون الذكاء الاصطناعي على بياناتنا؟

لا بشكل افتراضي. الوضع الافتراضي حتمي (deterministic).

entries.title: Enterprise Buyer FAQ — Dealix
entries.preview:
# Enterprise Buyer FAQ — Dealix

## What is Dealix?

A Saudi-first company that builds business operating systems on top of the tools you already use: CRM, WhatsApp, reviews, reports, billing. We don't replace your stack — we connect, govern, and produce proof.

## How is Dealix different from a marketing agency?

Agencies run campaigns. Dealix builds an operating system that outlives the contract: flows, dashboards, approvals, proof reports. Not a one-off campaign.

## Do you use AI on our data

entries.title: Dealix Service Level Boundaries
entries.preview:
# Dealix Service Level Boundaries

This is what we commit to. Anything outside this list is a discussion, not a default.

## Response windows (business hours, Riyadh)

| Tier | First response | Resolution window |
| --- | --- | --- |
| P0 (production down) | 1 business hour | same business day |
| P1 (workflow blocked) | 2 business hours | next business day |
| P2 (cosmetic / non-blocking) | 1 business day | 5 business days |
| P3 (enhancement / feature request) | acknowledged in 2 business days

entries.title: Dealix AI Governance Statement
entries.preview:
# Dealix AI Governance Statement

## Default mode is deterministic

Dealix's operator scripts and review tooling do not require an LLM to run. The default produces deterministic, reproducible output. LLM-assist is opt-in and per task.

## What we never do

- We do not auto-send AI-generated messages.
- We do not let AI write contractual or pricing language without founder review.
- We do not use customer data to train shared models.
- We do not claim AI-generated proof items.

## Where AI is all

Dealix builds business operating systems on top of the customer's existing tools (CRM, WhatsApp, reviews, reports, billing). It does not replace systems; it routes data, automates approvals, and produces proof.

entries.title: Implementation Assurance Plan
entries.preview:
# Implementation Assurance Plan

## Phase 1 — Diagnostic Sprint (7 days)

1. Day 1: Kickoff workshop. Map customer's existing CRM, WhatsApp, review, billing, and reporting flows. Output: in-scope/out-of-scope matrix.
2. Day 2-3: Workflow review. Founder + customer commercial owner walk live process. Output: friction log.
3. Day 4-5: Draft the chosen OS module (Revenue OS / Review OS / Command Center / etc.).
4. Day 6: Customer review session. Adjust scope, pricing, deliverables.
5. Day 7: Signed

entries.title: Legal Review Triggers
entries.preview:
# Legal Review Triggers

When Dealix engages outside legal counsel before signing.

## Always
- First engagement with a new enterprise (revenue > 100M SAR/year).
- Multi-tenant deals.
- Government / semi-government entities.
- Any deal with non-standard MSA terms.

## Often
- Indemnity clauses outside the standard MSA outline.
- Liability caps below 12-month fees.
- Data residency requirements outside KSA.
- Sub-processor restrictions.

## Sometimes
- IP assignment changes from default (customer

entries.title: Contract Handoff Checklist
entries.preview:
# Contract Handoff Checklist

Before a quote becomes a signed SOW:

## Customer-side
- [ ] Decision-maker named and email confirmed.
- [ ] Billing contact named.
- [ ] Customer's legal review status known (waived / pending / required).
- [ ] Customer's procurement process disclosed (any portal required?).
- [ ] PO number (if applicable) noted.

## Dealix-side
- [ ] Quote `approved` in `quotes.index.json`.
- [ ] Offer-specific SOW filled from `business/contracts/STATEMENT_OF_WORK_TEMPLATE_*.md`.

entries.title: Quote Approval Policy
entries.preview:
# Quote Approval Policy

## Quote lifecycle
1. **draft** — Generated by `scripts/generate_quote.py`. Not yet reviewed.
2. **pending_review** — Default state after generation.
3. **approved** — `scripts/approve_quote.py` with a reviewer name.
4. **sent** — `scripts/mark_quote_sent.py` records the manual send via customer channel.
5. **accepted** — Customer signed. Moves to `deals.ledger.json`.
6. **rejected** | **expired** — Closed; reason captured.

## SLAs
- Quote must reach `approved` within 2

entries.title: Red Flags & Disqualification
entries.preview:
# Red Flags & Disqualification

## Hard disqualifiers (walk away)
- Customer demands outcome-contingent pricing ("pay only if X happens").
- Customer wants Dealix to send cold WhatsApp / mass email at scale.
- Customer wants Dealix to bypass platform terms of service (scraping, fake reviews, fake clicks).
- Customer wants Dealix to manage their financial / legal decisions autonomously.
- Customer cannot name a single decision-maker.
- Customer has hostile internal politics that block weekly revi

entries.title: Dealix Deal Desk Rules
entries.preview:
# Dealix Deal Desk Rules

## Purpose
The deal desk is the founder-operated gate between a qualified opportunity and a signed SOW.

## When the deal desk runs
- Any opportunity above 5,000 SAR setup or 2,000 SAR/mo MRR.
- Any custom scope outside the published 17 offerings.
- Any discount request below the floor in `PRICING_DISCOUNT_POLICY.md`.
- Any acceptance-criteria deviation.

## Inputs
- `business/_data/scored_leads.json` — qualified account.
- `business/_data/quotes.index.json` — pending q

entries.title: Change Request Policy
entries.preview:
# Change Request Policy

## Definition
Any customer-initiated request that changes scope, timeline, or price after a SOW is signed.

## Process
1. Customer raises request in writing (email or `/client-portal/[id]`).
2. Founder estimates effort within 2 business days.
3. Founder issues a Change Order (CO) — a mini-quote attached to the original SOW.
4. Customer approves CO before work begins.
5. CO is logged in `business/_data/deals.ledger.json` with `parentDealId`.

## What counts as scope creep

entries.title: Master Service Agreement (MSA) — Outline
entries.preview:
# Master Service Agreement (MSA) — Outline

**Operational scaffold. Not legal advice.** Retain qualified counsel before signing.

## 1. Parties
Dealix SA ("Provider") and `[Customer legal name]` ("Customer").

## 2. Services
Provider will deliver business operating system services as detailed in one or more Statements of Work ("SOW") executed under this MSA.

## 3. Term
Effective from the Effective Date until terminated by either party with 30 days written notice. Active SOWs survive termination

entries.title: Client responsibilities
entries.preview:
# Client responsibilities

A successful Dealix engagement requires the customer to bring four things.

## 1. A named commercial owner
- One person who can approve drafts, accept deliverables, and decide priorities.
- Time commitment: ~90 min/week for the weekly review + ad-hoc Slack/WhatsApp for draft approvals.
- This is not delegable to an assistant.

## 2. Access to the customer's existing tools
- CRM (read access at minimum, write for the Managed Ops tier).
- WhatsApp Business or shared inbo

entries.title: Proof item schema
entries.preview:
# Proof item schema

```json
{
  "id": "p-...",
  "title": "Plain-language statement (e.g., 'Lead response time dropped from 8h to 90min')",
  "evidence": "Where this is observable (e.g., 'CRM response_time field, weekly average week-23 2026')",
  "date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "customerConsent": true,
  "publicShareable": false,
  "demo": false
}
```

Rules:
- `evidence` must be specific enough that a third party could verify.
- `customerConsent=true` required before any external use.
- `publicShareab

entries.title: Proof Vault (Dealix)
entries.preview:
# Proof Vault (Dealix)

## الهدف
كل ما يثبت أن Dealix شغّال لازم يكون موثّق:
- ملاحظة أسبوعية
- تقرير شهري
- دراسة حالة (بعد 90 يوم)
- توسيع ناجح

## أين
- `business/_data/proof_vault.json`

## قواعد
- لا fake case study
- لا fake testimonial
- demo data لازم يكون موسوم demo
- توثيق فقط بعد موافقة العميل

## الربط بالـ pipeline
- كل proof item → account
- كل expansion offer → proof item
- كل case study → account + period

entries.title: Retainer Expansion Playbook
entries.preview:
# Retainer Expansion Playbook

## When to start the conversation
Month 2 weekly review, after two healthy proof items are in the vault.

## Expansion paths
1. **Adjacent OS module**: customer on Revenue OS → add Review OS or Delivery OS.
2. **Tier up**: Managed Ops basic → Managed Ops pro.
3. **Headcount**: same module, expanded to another business unit.
4. **Custom**: ad-hoc engagement that becomes a new SOW.

## Anti-patterns
- Don't pitch expansion before the first proof item.
- Don't bundle

entries.title: Client Success Signals
entries.preview:
# Client Success Signals

Quantitative and qualitative indicators that a customer is healthy or at risk.

## Healthy
- Weekly review attended without escalation.
- Approval SLA met (<= 2 business days response).
- Customer-driven new feature requests.
- Renewal conversation initiated by the customer.
- Referrals offered.

## At risk
- 2+ skipped weekly reviews.
- Approval SLA missed.
- Customer says "we'll think about it" on basic deliverables.
- Customer asks for discount without scope change.

entries.title: AI Output Quality Bar
entries.preview:
# AI Output Quality Bar

Before any output is forwarded to the founder for review, it must clear:

## Content checks
- [ ] No banned claims (`scripts/lib/ai_eval.check_no_banned_claims`).
- [ ] No autosend language (`check_no_autosend`).
- [ ] review_status = pending_human_review.

## Tone checks
- AR: Saudi business register, no colloquial slang in customer-facing artifacts.
- EN: executive register, ≤ 100 words for first-touch outreach.

## Truthfulness checks
- Every number traces to a source

entries.title: Banned Claims Policy
entries.preview:
# Banned Claims Policy

## Hard ban (refuse and log)
- "Guaranteed X" or "X guaranteed"
- "نضمن" + variants
- "100% money-back" + variants
- "We promise results"
- "ROI guaranteed"
- "Risk-free outcome"
- "Pay only if you win"
- Any fake testimonial / case study

## Soft ban (flag for founder edit)
- Numbers without source attribution.
- Aspirational language ("revolutionary", "best-in-class").
- "Industry-leading" without comparison data.
- "Most companies" without citation.

## Enforcement
- `

entries.title: Prompt Versioning Policy
entries.preview:
# Prompt Versioning Policy

## Versioning
- Prompts are versioned: v1, v2, ... in the file name or as a header.
- A prompt change that changes the output meaningfully bumps the version.
- The old version is kept for audit reproducibility.

## Promoting a prompt
1. Author proposes a new version in a PR.
2. Author runs `scripts/run_ai_evals.py --mode demo` showing the new version passes all evals.
3. Author updates `PROMPT_REGISTRY.md`.
4. Reviewer signs off.

## Deprecating
- A prompt is deprecat

entries.title: AI Task Routing (Dealix)
entries.preview:
# AI Task Routing (Dealix)

## نموذج التوجيه
| Task | Route | Reason |
|------|-------|--------|
| Lead scoring | Deterministic script | Audit + reproducibility |
| Outreach draft | LLM (draft only) | Tone + language |
| Proposal generation | LLM + template | Quality + speed |
| Proof report | LLM + template | Quality + speed |
| Pricing quote | Deterministic script | Audit + math |
| Risk flag | Deterministic script | Logic + audit |
| Final send | Human only | Safety |

## قواعد التوجيه
- لا r

entries.title: Prompt: Outreach Draft (English, v1)
entries.preview:
# Prompt: Outreach Draft (English, v1)

Role: Dealix founder.
Task: Write a first-touch WhatsApp or email message to a commercial owner at a Saudi B2B company, anchored on a visible friction signal.

Rules:
- No guarantees, no "outcome" claims.
- No unsourced metrics.
- Respectful, no flattery.
- Close with a 7-day workflow review offer.
- ≤ 100 words.
- review_status = pending_human_review.

entries.title: Prompt: Translation AR ↔ EN (v1)
entries.preview:
# Prompt: Translation AR ↔ EN (v1)

Translate the given text from one language to the other.

Rules:
- Preserve technical terms (CRM, OS, MRR, etc.).
- Preserve any Dealix offering names verbatim.
- Preserve currency notation (SAR).
- Do NOT translate Dealix brand voice into machine-translation style; keep founder voice.
- Output: target-language translation only. No explanations.

entries.title: Prompt: Sales Call Summary (v1)
entries.preview:
# Prompt: Sales Call Summary (v1)

Read raw call notes (founder-provided). Produce:
1. 5-bullet summary of what was discussed.
2. Buyer's stated pain (verbatim quotes).
3. Identified next step (with owner + date).
4. Open questions / blockers.
5. Recommended Dealix offer match.

Rules:
- Quote buyer verbatim where possible.
- No invented intent.
- AR + EN.

entries.title: Prompt: Proposal Section (English, v1)
entries.preview:
# Prompt: Proposal Section (English, v1)

Write a section of a Dealix proposal. Section name is in the input: executive summary, scope, deliverables, pricing, risks, close.

Rules:
- No fabricated numbers.
- Pricing from the published catalog; no discount without approval.
- Include acceptance criteria per deliverable.
- Concise, executive English.

entries.title: Prompt: Compliance Review (v1)
entries.preview:
# Prompt: Compliance Review (v1)

Read a draft outbound message or proposal. Flag:
- Banned claims (guarantees, fake metrics).
- Missing scope clarifications.
- Implied auto-send language.
- Pricing inconsistencies vs. catalog.

Output: a checklist of issues with file:line references. Do NOT rewrite the content; only flag.

entries.title: Prompt: Weakness Hypothesis (v1)
entries.preview:
# Prompt: Weakness Hypothesis (v1)

Role: Dealix workflow analyst.
Task: Given an account's visibleSignal and segment, propose 1-3 weakness hypotheses tied to a Dealix OS module.

Output rules:
- Anchor in observable signals only.
- No insinuations about the customer's internal team.
- Map each hypothesis to one of: Revenue OS, Review OS, Delivery OS, Command Center, Client Acquisition OS.
- End with the single best 7-day diagnostic experiment to test the top hypothesis.

entries.title: Prompt: Outreach Draft (Arabic, v1)
entries.preview:
# Prompt: Outreach Draft (Arabic, v1)

Role: مؤسس Dealix.
Task: اكتب رسالة واتساب أو إيميل مبدأية لمسؤول تجاري في شركة سعودية، بناءً على إشارة احتكاك ملاحظة.

قواعد:
- لا ضمانات ولا "نضمن".
- لا ادعاءات أرقام بدون مصدر.
- نبرة محترمة بلا تملق.
- تنتهي بدعوة لمراجعة workflow بـ 7 أيام.
- ≤ 100 كلمة.
- review_status = pending_human_review.

entries.title: Prompt: Proof Report Summary (v1)
entries.preview:
# Prompt: Proof Report Summary (v1)

Summarize a customer's weekly proof items into a 4-sentence narrative.

Rules:
- Cite each proof item's evidence source.
- No claims beyond what's in the proof items.
- AR + EN paragraph each.
- review_status = pending_human_review.

entries.title: Prompt: Objection Response (English, v1)
entries.preview:
# Prompt: Objection Response (English, v1)

Read the objection. Produce a short response that respects the buyer.

Pattern:
1. Acknowledge.
2. Reframe with facts.
3. Offer low-risk next step (Diagnostic Sprint at 499 SAR).
4. Open close question.

entries.title: Prompt: Client Status Summary (v1)
entries.preview:
# Prompt: Client Status Summary (v1)

Given a workspace JSON, produce a 3-paragraph status summary covering: what was shipped, what was found, what's next.

Rules:
- No projections beyond next deliverable's due date.
- Cite each deliverable by id.
- AR + EN versions.
- review_status = pending_human_review.

entries.title: Knowledge Retention Policy
entries.preview:
# Knowledge Retention Policy

- Dealix general knowledge: retained indefinitely; reviewed quarterly.
- Customer knowledge: retained for the SOW lifetime + 30 days.
- AI audit log: 12 months.
- Deletion on customer request honored within 14 business days.

entries.title: Client Knowledge Boundaries
entries.preview:
# Client Knowledge Boundaries

- Dealix's general knowledge base is shared across customers.
- Customer-specific knowledge (their CRM data, transcripts, configs) is per-tenant only.
- Per-tenant knowledge never crosses into shared indexes.
- Per-tenant knowledge is deleted within 30 days of SOW termination.

entries.title: RAG Architecture Plan
entries.preview:
# RAG Architecture Plan

## V14 (today)
- Deterministic local index. No embeddings.
- Substring + keyword match on titles + first 500 chars.
- Good enough for ~1,000 source files.

## V15+ (future)
- Embedding-backed vector store (provider TBD).
- Per-tenant collections.
- Reranking with a small reranker.
- Hybrid retrieval (BM25 + dense).

## Quality gates before flipping to vector RAG
- Embedding provider contracted with data-residency clause.
- Per-tenant isolation tested.
- Eval set: at leas

entries.title: Dealix Knowledge Index (operator reference)
entries.preview:
# Dealix Knowledge Index (operator reference)

Build the index:
```
python3 scripts/index_knowledge_sources.py --demo
```

Search:
```
python3 scripts/search_knowledge_base.py --query "Revenue OS"
```

Generate a pack for a topic:
```
python3 scripts/generate_knowledge_pack.py
```

Files indexed: see `business/_data/knowledge_index.json` after building.

entries.title: Dealix Knowledge Base System
entries.preview:
# Dealix Knowledge Base System

## Purpose
Make Dealix's own playbooks, FAQ, and policies searchable for the founder and (later) for the customer-facing assistant.

## Sources
- `business/enterprise/*.md`
- `business/deal-desk/*.md`
- `business/contracts/*.md`
- `business/proof/*.md`
- `business/ai/*.md`
- `docs/**/*.md`

## Index format (v1)
Deterministic local index in `business/_data/knowledge_index.json`. Maps each markdown file to its title and first 500 chars. Search is substring + lowerca

entries.title: Source Citation Policy
entries.preview:
# Source Citation Policy

- Every AI-generated artifact that quotes a source must cite the file path.
- Citations look like: `_(source: business/proof/CLIENT_SUCCESS_SIGNALS.md)_`.
- If no source can be cited, the artifact carries the phrase "general guidance — verify before customer use".

entries.title: Client Kickoff SOP
entries.preview:
# Client Kickoff SOP

## Day -2
- Send kickoff confirmation + agenda + customer responsibilities checklist.
- Confirm attendees (commercial owner + operator).

## Day 0 (kickoff)
1. **Intro (10 min):** scope, goals, definitions.
2. **In-scope / out-of-scope matrix (20 min):** write it down live.
3. **Access grant (15 min):** customer adds Dealix to CRM (read), Google Business (admin if Review OS), WhatsApp Business (operator role).
4. **Friction interview (30 min):** what's costing them money to

entries.title: Delivery Risk Register
entries.preview:
# Delivery Risk Register

Categories of risks and how to handle each.

## Access risk
Symptom: Customer hasn't granted access to the agreed system.
Action: Pause dependent deliverables. Escalate at week 2.

## Scope ambiguity
Symptom: Customer asks for things "while we're at it".
Action: Convert to a CO (`CHANGE_REQUEST_POLICY.md`).

## Capacity risk
Symptom: Multiple customers want the same slot.
Action: Stagger weekly reviews. Don't double-book Sundays.

## Reputation risk
Symptom: Customer wa

entries.title: Weekly Client Review Rhythm
entries.preview:
# Weekly Client Review Rhythm

## When
Same slot every week, agreed at kickoff. 45 min default.

## Agenda
1. **What we shipped** (10 min) — completed deliverables.
2. **What we found** (10 min) — friction log updates.
3. **What we'll ship next** (10 min) — next 1-2 deliverables.
4. **What we need from you** (10 min) — open approvals, access, data.
5. **Open risks** (5 min) — review the risk register.

## Output
- Notes saved to `business/proof/exports/<client-id>/weekly-<date>.md`.
- Proof item

entries.title: Deliverable Acceptance Flow
entries.preview:
# Deliverable Acceptance Flow

1. **Draft** — Founder produces, demo-marks if applicable.
2. **Internal review** — Founder walks through against `ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA_TEMPLATE.md`.
3. **Delivery** — Sent to commercial owner with a clear "by Friday EOD, please confirm" ask.
4. **Customer review** — Customer accepts, rejects with notes, or asks for changes.
5. **Iterate** — Up to two iteration rounds in scope; further iterations become a CO.
6. **Accept** — Recorded via `record_client_approval.py`.

entries.title: Approval Gate Policy
entries.preview:
# Approval Gate Policy

Customer-facing items requiring written approval before going live.

| Item | Approver | Tool |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Persuasion angle batch | Commercial owner | `request_client_approval.py` |
| WhatsApp draft batch | Commercial owner | `approve_outreach_draft.py` |
| Workflow change in production | Operator + Commercial owner | `request_client_approval.py` |
| First proof report | Commercial owner | `record_client_approval.py` |
| Public case study draft | Commercial own

entries.title: Delivery Workspace System
entries.preview:
# Delivery Workspace System

## Purpose
Make delivery visible to the customer, structured for the founder, and auditable.

- `client_portal.demo.json` — demo seed.
- `/client-portal/[id]` — customer-facing surface.

  - `create_client_workspace.py`
  - `add_deliverable.py`
  - `mark_deliverable_done.py`
  - `request_client_approval.py`
  - `record_client_approval.py`
  - `generate_client_status_r

entries.title: سيناريو العرض التوضيحي — Dealix (عربي)
entries.preview:
# سيناريو العرض التوضيحي — Dealix (عربي)

مدة: 20 دقيقة. مستمع: مسؤول تجاري / مالك شركة B2B سعودية.

## (1) دقيقتان — الافتتاحية

"Dealix يبني نظام تشغيل تجاري حقيقي فوق أدواتك الموجودة. مش CRM جديد، مش وكالة. نظام يومي يقلب تشتيت المبيعات والواتساب والسمعة إلى قرارات قابلة للقياس."

## (2) 3 دقائق — لوحة Command Center

افتح `/command-center`:
- Top accounts بتقديرات.
- Drafts الواتساب الجاهزة للمراجعة.
- المتابعات المستحقة اليوم.
- إثباتات الأسبوع.

ركّز: "كل شيء معد بشري قبل ما يطلع منك."

##

entries.title: Demo Close
entries.preview:
# Demo Close

Closing sequence after the 20-min walkthrough.

## Step 1 — Confirm
"Before we wrap, did you see at least one part of your workflow you'd want to fix in the next 30 days?"

If yes → Step 2. If no → respectful close, send the FAQ, ask permission to follow up in 60 days.

## Step 2 — Offer
"I'd like to propose the Diagnostic Sprint. It's 7 days, 499 SAR. By day 7 you have a written friction log, a tailored data pack proposal, and the first proof report. If you don't see 5x value in t

entries.title: Live Workflow Review Script
entries.preview:
# Live Workflow Review Script

Used during the 7-day diagnostic sprint, day 2-3 workshop.

## Setup
- 90-min slot.
- Founder + customer's commercial owner + 1 operator who actually does the work.
- Screen share their CRM, WhatsApp, and review console live.

## Sequence

### Block 1 (15 min) — Lead arrival
- Where do leads land?
- Who sees them first?
- How long until first response?
- Tag every friction visible on screen.

### Block 2 (15 min) — Qualification
- Who qualifies? What rule?
- Show 5

entries.title: Demo Q&A — Common Objections + Responses
entries.preview:
# Demo Q&A — Common Objections + Responses

## "We already have a CRM."
Good. Dealix sits on top. We don't replace it. We connect, govern, and produce proof on top of HubSpot / Salesforce / Zoho / Bitrix / Pipedrive.

## "We tried an agency, didn't work."
Agencies run campaigns; Dealix builds a system that outlives the contract. Start with a 499 SAR diagnostic sprint — no commitment.

## "ChatGPT can do this."
ChatGPT generates text. Dealix runs a daily operator pack, queues drafts for human rev

entries.title: Dealix Demo Script (English)
entries.preview:
# Dealix Demo Script (English)

Length: 20 minutes. Audience: B2B commercial owner / founder.

## (1) 2 min — Opening

"Dealix builds a real business operating system on top of the tools you already use. Not a new CRM, not an agency. A daily system that converts scattered sales, WhatsApp, and reputation into measurable decisions."

## (2) 3 min — Command Center dashboard

Open `/command-center`:
- Top scored accounts.
- WhatsApp drafts pending review.
- Follow-ups due today.
- This week's proof

entries.title: Founder Demo Flow — Internal Reference
entries.preview:
# Founder Demo Flow — Internal Reference

Step-by-step the founder follows during a live demo.

## Before the call
1. Open `/command-center` in tab 1.
2. Open `/revenue-machine` in tab 2.
3. Open `/daily-draft` in tab 3.
4. Open `/proof-vault` in tab 4.
5. Open `/enterprise-readiness` in tab 5.
6. Have `LIVE_WORKFLOW_REVIEW_SCRIPT.md` open on second screen.

## During the call
- Speak the Arabic script if the buyer is Arabic-first, English otherwise.
- After every section, ask: "هل هذا قريب من ت

entries.title: V14 Agent Operations
entries.preview:
# V14 Agent Operations

## Design principles
1. **Deterministic by default.** No external call required to produce output.
2. **LLM-assist is opt-in.** `--use-ai` flag + provider env var.
3. **Fail-safe.** Provider failure → deterministic fallback with metadata tagging.
4. **Auditable.** Every call records provider, model, prompt version, review_status.
5. **Banned-claim filtered.** Pre-call safety check refuses unsafe prompts.

## Modules
- `scripts/lib/ai_router.py` — the routing entry point.

entries.title: AI Model Router Plan (Dealix)
entries.preview:
# AI Model Router Plan (Dealix)

## Goals
- Single router for all LLM calls in Dealix
- Cost + latency + quality + safety observable
- Easy to swap providers
- Demo mode works without API keys

## Components
- `dealix/ai/router.py` — central router
- `dealix/ai/providers/base.py` — provider interface
- `dealix/ai/providers/openai.py` — OpenAI provider
- `dealix/ai/providers/anthropic.py` — Anthropic provider
- `dealix/ai/providers/local.py` — local stub for demo

## Routing table
| Task class |

entries.title: AI Audit Logging
entries.preview:
# AI Audit Logging

## Log file
`business/_data/ai_audit_log.json` (append-only).

## Per entry
- timestamp
- task
- provider
- model
- prompt_version
- review_status
- deterministic (bool)
- safety_passed (bool)
- note
- reviewer (if approved)

## What we never log
- Raw customer data inside the prompt body (in production mode).
- API keys.
- Personally identifying info beyond what the customer's SOW authorizes.

## Retention
- 12 months by default.
- Customer-specific entries deleted within 30

entries.title: Deterministic Fallback Policy
entries.preview:
# Deterministic Fallback Policy

## Why
- Service must work in a permission-denied / offline / quota-exhausted environment.
- Customer who hasn't opted into LLM-assist still gets a usable artifact.
- Auditable output that doesn't depend on a third party.

## What "deterministic" means
- Same input → same output every run.
- Output is a structured placeholder for the operator to edit.
- Output never claims a result.
- Output carries `deterministic: true` in metadata.

## When fallback triggers
-

entries.preview:

Default: deterministic. Opt-in routing per task is below.

| Task | Preferred provider | Fallback chain |
| --- | --- | --- |
| outreach_draft (AR) | MiniMax | Kimi → deterministic |
| outreach_draft (EN) | OpenAI | Kimi → deterministic |
| proposal_section | OpenAI | Kimi → deterministic |
| objection_response | Kimi | OpenAI → deterministic |
| translation_ar_en | MiniMax | Kimi → deterministic |
| proof_report_summary | DeepSeek | OpenAI → deterministic |
| client_status_su

entries.title: AI Review Gates
entries.preview:
# AI Review Gates

Every AI-touched artifact passes these gates before any external use.

## Gate 1 — Safety check (pre-call)
- Refuse banned phrases (guarantee, scrape, fake review, etc.).
- Refuse oversized prompts.
- Refuse if customer hasn't opted into LLM-assist.

## Gate 2 — Eval check (post-call)
- No banned claims in output.
- No autosend language.
- review_status set to `pending_human_review`.

## Gate 3 — Human review
- Founder reads, edits, approves.
- Approval recorded in `business/_

entries.title: Model Provider Matrix
entries.preview:
# Model Provider Matrix

| Provider | Env var | Strengths | Cautions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| MiniMax | `MINIMAX_API_KEY` | Long context, good Arabic | Throttling |
| Kimi | `KIMI_API_KEY` | Strong reasoning | Cost |
| DeepSeek | `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` | Code + reasoning | Newer ecosystem |
| OpenRouter | `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` | Multi-model fallback | Per-route reliability |
| OpenAI | `OPENAI_API_KEY` | General quality | Cost, regional latency |

Routing rules (when activated):
- AR-heavy outre

entries.title: Production Auth Requirements
entries.preview:
# Production Auth Requirements

## Hard requirements before going to production

1. **IdP wired**: OIDC or SAML, with customer-supplied IdP for customer-facing tenants.
2. **MFA enforced** on every admin login.
3. **Session timeout** ≤ 12 hours of inactivity.
4. **Privileged actions** logged: who, what, when, what changed.
5. **`NEXT_PUBLIC_DEMO_MODE`** is unset in production.
6. **`DEALIX_ADMIN_PASSWORD`** is unset; password-only access is not allowed.
7. **Secrets** loaded from secret manager,

These pages contain operational data and should not be served to the public web in production:

- `/launch`
- `/client-portal/*` (except `/client-portal/demo`)

## Demo mode

When `NEXT_PUBLIC_DEMO_MODE=true`, internal routes render with **DEMO** badges a

entries.title: ZATCA-aware invoicing notes
entries.preview:
# ZATCA-aware invoicing notes

## Position
Dealix is NOT a tax invoice provider. ZATCA-compliant invoices are issued by the customer's accounting system. Dealix's invoice stubs are operational placeholders only.

## What customers actually need
- A KSA-licensed accounting system (Zoho Books, Wafeq, ERPNext + KSA add-ons, SAP Business One with KSA localization, etc.).
- ZATCA Fatoora portal credentials.
- Phase-1 / Phase-2 e-invoicing integration as required for their entity.
- A finance owner wh

entries.title: Payment security boundaries
entries.preview:
# Payment security boundaries

## Hard rules
- Dealix code never stores PAN (primary account number).
- Dealix code never logs PAN.
- Dealix code never receives webhook payloads with PAN.
- Dealix code never proxies payment forms.

## Stubs
`integrations/payments/*_stub.py` exist to:
- Define the function signatures.
- Document the env vars required for live activation.
- Make the integration plan reviewable in PRs.

They do NOT call any provider.

## Activation gates
Before flipping a stub to a

entries.title: Stripe integration plan (future)
entries.preview:
# Stripe integration plan (future)

## When to use Stripe instead of Moyasar
- Customer pays in non-SAR currency.
- Customer's existing finance stack already uses Stripe.
- Cross-border subscription with international customer.

## Scope (when activated)
- Issue Stripe Payment Link or Checkout Session for approved quote.
- Webhook on `payment_intent.succeeded` → mark deal won.
- Webhook on `customer.subscription.deleted` → mark customer churned in CRM.

## Out of scope
- Storing card details.
-

entries.title: Payment architecture
entries.preview:
# Payment architecture

Dealix does NOT process payments. The customer's accounting system does. This document explains the boundary.

## Status today (V12)

- No PCI scope.
- No card data handled.
- No payment links generated automatically.
- Stubs in `integrations/payments/` show the intended contract; they do not call any provider.

## When a customer pays

1. Founder issues an invoice from the customer's accounting system (Zoho, QuickBooks, Wafeq, etc.).
2. Customer pays via their normal cha

entries.title: Moyasar integration plan (future)
entries.preview:
# Moyasar integration plan (future)

## Why Moyasar
- Saudi-licensed payment processor.
- ZATCA / Mada / Apple Pay support.
- Strong API + webhook docs.

## Scope (when activated)
- Generate a payment link for an approved quote.
- Receive a webhook on payment.
- Mark the deal `won` in `deals.ledger.json`.

## Out of scope
- Storing card details.
- Refund automation.
- Subscription billing (use Moyasar subscriptions only after dedicated security review).

## Prerequisites before going live
- Sign
